evidence_quotes.quote: unilaterally remove this video game or service remotely from the device in which it is installed.
its servers were switched off when the latter ceased to be profitable.

paragraphs.text: He believes that the Digital Fairness Act would have to be able to cover also in the event that a company distributing a video game or service can unilaterally remove this video game or service remotely from the device in which it is installed. In my personal case, I have seen it passed 2 times with video games I have paid for. The first went with the video game of the company Blizzard, Overwatch, who decided to make a new Overwatch2, with an even more service-oriented approach, launched it as Free to Play, and removed the original from the players’ game bookshop. They cannot be played in any way, so that they do not compete with themselves. The second video game I got with The Crew, published by the company Ubisoft. Originally launched as a game as a service, where internet use was mandatory, although it had a campaign for a single player, it was removed, while its servers were switched off when the latter ceased to be profitable. There is now no trace that you bought and paid for these games, as they have been removed remotely from my computer. I do not ask for games to be kept alive always, but I want companies to have end-of-life plans so that they are not lost, and they cannot make all the money that we have invested in them flourish as if they are no longer profitable. What I am asking is for these plans to take into account users and players, allowing them to access offline versions of the video games or services they have purchased, with the corresponding restrictions, or to release the way to create private servers of such games, so that users can continue to enjoy them, and over time, they can continue to be remembered and studied in order to improve the new video games and services being created. What I have mentioned is what you are trying to move with the citizens’ initiative of Stop Destroying Videogames, and I would be grateful if you could take this point into account. Thank you for your attention.
